intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to a voice that validates their frustration through humor and irreverence rather than cheerful encouragement
facade.label: Sarcastic tone
facade.acceptImplies: App should use witty copy, dark humor about cooking failure, and a knowing tone that sides with the user against the kitchen
facade.rejectImplies: App should use positive, encouraging, straightforward language that frames cooking as achievable and rewarding

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to a voice that validates their frustration through humor and irreverence rather than cheerful encouragement
evidence.implication: App should use witty copy, dark humor about cooking failure, and a knowing tone that sides with the user against the kitchen

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer forgiving, approachable UI with minimal sharp corners—design that feels inviting rather than demanding.
facade.axisTargeted: Friendliness vs. Severity (rounded/organic shapes vs. sharp/geometric)
facade.label: Soft rounded buttons
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace curves, soft shadows, and playful proportions. Reduce intimidation through visual warmth.
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer crisp, efficient, modern aesthetics—clean lines and structure over comfort-forward design.

facade.hypothesis: People who hate cooking prefer seeing calorie count or prep time upfront rather than reading full ingredient lists
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs key metrics — showing a single anchor number vs. comprehensive information
facade.label: One-number recipes
facade.acceptImplies: Recipe discovery should lead with the ONE number that matters most (time, calories, servings) — metric-first design
facade.rejectImplies: Users want to see the full picture before deciding — narrative context and ingredient details matter more than a speed metric

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking want minimal friction — they prefer pre-filtered, single-action recipe access over browsing or decision-making
facade.axisTargeted: Interaction complexity: multi-step discovery vs instant access
facade.label: One-tap recipes
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward automation, pre-selected defaults, and single-action patterns (floating buttons, quick-pick cards, pre-loaded favorites)
facade.rejectImplies: Users want agency and control — design for browsing, filtering, and deliberate selection even if it adds steps

facade.hypothesis: Users feel less overwhelmed if recipe ingredients load sequentially with slight pauses rather than all at once
facade.axisTargeted: Animation and reveal pacing—gradual unfold vs instant dump
facade.label: Ingredients appear one by one
facade.acceptImplies: Staggered, animated reveals reduce cognitive load for cooking-averse users; build into ingredient lists, instructions, timer countdowns
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer instant, scannable content over choreographed reveals; motion may feel patronizing or slow for quick-reference cooking tasks

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking might prefer imperfect, casual visual language over styled food porn—reducing intimidation and signaling 'this is easy, not precious'
facade.axisTargeted: Organic vs Digital; Intimate vs Expansive
facade.label: Blurry food photos
facade.acceptImplies: Design should feel handmade, forgiving, anti-glossy. Real textures over polished renders. Accessibility signal: you don't need to be a perfectionist.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want polished, aspirational visuals that make them *feel* like confident cooks despite hating the process. Design should hide the mess, not celebrate it.

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity/minimalism vs. rich imagery/inspiration; reveals if they're intimidated by food photos or motivated by them
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided flow vs. ingredient-first or freestyle browsing; reveals navigation comfort level for cooking-averse users
Whether user responds to encouraging/cheerful tone vs. no-nonsense/sarcastic tone; reveals if they need motivation or just want efficiency
Whether user wants to feel capable/empowered vs. entertained/amused; reveals if cooking feels like a chore to overcome or a game to enjoy
Whether user wants ingredient lists upfront vs. time/difficulty badges vs. substitution options; reveals what information removes friction most
Whether user prefers instant results/quick animations vs. slow reveals/dramatic builds; reveals patience level and desire for snappy feedback

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity/minimalism vs. rich imagery/inspiration; reveals if they're intimidated by food photos or motivated by them
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided flow vs. ingredient-first or freestyle browsing; reveals navigation comfort level for cooking-averse users
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/cheerful tone vs. no-nonsense/sarcastic tone; reveals if they need motivation or just want efficiency
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants to feel capable/empowered vs. entertained/amused; reveals if cooking feels like a chore to overcome or a game to enjoy
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants ingredient lists upfront vs. time/difficulty badges vs. substitution options; reveals what information removes friction most
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant results/quick animations vs. slow reveals/dramatic builds; reveals patience level and desire for snappy feedback
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking will appreciate a voice that acknowledges cooking is annoying and tedious rather than pretending it's fun or aspirational
facade.label: Brutally honest
facade.acceptImplies: Double down on anti-cooking sentiment; use copy that validates annoyance, calls out cooking myths, speaks like someone who'd rather be doing anything else
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer encouragement or at least neutral framing; tone should be helpful and supportive rather than cynical; avoid reinforcing negativity about the activity itself

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers dead-simple meals in under 15 minutes with minimal ingredients and zero fuss.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Meals so simple, you'll actually cook them

Today's Picks
Garlic Butter Pasta

Sheet Pan Chicken
12 min • 5 ingredients
Egg Fried Rice
10 min • 4 ingredients
Why QuickBite
10
min max
5
